summary: The CI audit job failed because `pnpm audit` detected a moderate severity vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (versions <=10.1.0), which has an XSS issue in Address6 HTML-emitting methods. The vulnerability is present in the dependency chain `network__fetch>@pnpm/network.agent>@pnpm/network.proxy-agent>socks-proxy-agent>socks>ip-address`. The process exited with code 1, failing the job.
root_cause: `pnpm audit` found 1 moderate vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (<=10.1.0) via the path `network__fetch>@pnpm/network.agent>@pnpm/network.proxy-agent>socks-proxy-agent>socks>ip-address`, causing the audit command to exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods
evidence.reason: This is the vulnerability description reported by pnpm audit that triggered the failure.
Identifies the range of affected ip-address versions currently in use.
Confirms a fix is available by upgrading ip-address to 10.1.1 or later.
Shows the full dependency path through which the vulnerable package is introduced.
Confirms pnpm audit detected exactly one vulnerability, causing it to exit non-zero.
Confirms the audit command exited with a non-zero code, which failed the CI job.

suggested_fix: Upgrade the `ip-address` transitive dependency to >=10.1.1 (patched version). Update the lockfile by bumping `socks` or another ancestor in the chain `socks-proxy-agent>socks>ip-address` to a version that depends on ip-address >=10.1.1, then commit the updated `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
